methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $37M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Nabla Bio is a Series A biotech company founded by Surge Biswas and Frances Anastassacos that has raised $37M in equity plus $1.5B+ in partnerships. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 11 comparable companies: 3 public-market biotech firms (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private-stage peers including Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, New Limit, Asimov, and Profluent, all in Biotech & Health at Series B or adjacent stages.

Two quantitative methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $37M raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $74M. Peer-median triangulation across 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables produced a median valuation of $805M (range: $100M–$3.3B). Public-comp anchoring proved inapplicable due to unavailable revenue data for the private target.

The two applicable methods produce a wide range: stage-progression implies $74M while peer-median implies $805M. The triangulated analysis supports a valuation band of $63M–$926M with a median of $440M. The material divergence reflects methodological tension between early-stage capital-based valuation and current peer-market pricing in a high-growth, pre-revenue biotech context. Key uncertainties include asset-stage maturity relative to comparables and the degree to which partnership value translates to equity valuation.
